commit.message:
fix(configure-live): create /var/usrlocal before using /usr/local

/usr/local is a symlink to ../var/usrlocal (bootc layout). bootc-rootfs.sh
wipes /var so the symlink target is missing. mkdir -p through a broken
symlink returns exit 1. Recreate /var/home and /var/usrlocal at script start.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat: add logic/ICD error reporting alongside spelling errors

Doctors can now report two types of issues:
- Spelling errors (yazim xetasi): corrected text for diagnosis/explanation fields
- Logic/ICD errors (mentiq xetasi): description of ICD coding problems

Admin panel shows both types with distinct badges and layouts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(vite): allowedHosts: true — allow all reverse-proxy hosts

Vite 8 blocks requests with non-matching Host header even with specific
allowedHosts list, because Tailscale Serve rewrites the Host header.
Using allowedHosts: true is safe here since host is bound to 127.0.0.1
(not accessible from external network directly).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add Supabase migration push workaround to copilot-instructions

Documents the duplicate 010 migration prefix issue and the
temporary rename workaround for future sessions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: load product.json at bootstrap to enable marketplace search

The Tauri bootstrap did not set _VSCODE_PRODUCT_JSON before importing
workbench modules, causing product.ts to fall through to a hardcoded
default without extensionsGallery configuration. This meant the
Extension Gallery service was never enabled, and marketplace search
returned no results.

Add a get_product_json Rust command that reads product.json and
package.json from the project root, and call it in the bootstrap
script to set the globals before any workbench module is loaded.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: switch imageService from cloudflare-binding to passthrough (#72)

* Initial plan

* fix: change imageService from cloudflare-binding to passthrough

The IMAGES Worker binding (env.IMAGES) is not provisioned — Cloudflare
Images is a paid add-on that has not been enabled for this account.
Using 'cloudflare-binding' caused env.IMAGES to be undefined at runtime,
which broke env.ASSETS.fetch() and produced a 'Page not found' fallback
on every request.

'passthrough' skips image optimisation entirely and passes images through
unchanged, which is the correct behaviour for a static site served from
Workers Assets where Cloudflare Images is not provisioned.

Updated the comment above imageService to document:
- why 'passthrough' is used
- what is required before switching to 'cloudflare-binding' in future

No IMAGES binding exists in src/types/env.ts — no change needed there.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/jaypatrick/bloqr-landing/sessions/8ff05464-2e8b-4c8b-b19a-bfd3def8773a

Co-authored-by: jaypatrick <1800595+jaypatrick@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: weekly free agent window with staged changes

Teams can open one free agent window per week (resets Saturday midnight PT).
While open, all adds/swaps are staged client-side. Only committed when the
user clicks Confirm — navigating away discards all staged changes.
Team-to-team trades remain unlimited and unaffected.

Backend:
- Migration 016: fa_window_used_at column + commit_free_agent_window RPC
  (SECURITY DEFINER, validates window not used this week, applies all
  swaps atomically with row locks, sets fa_window_used_at)
- New API route POST /api/leagues/private/free-agent-window/commit
- Shared utility lib/free-agent-window.ts for week boundary calculation

UI:
- FreeAgentsList reworked: Open Window → stage changes → Confirm/Discard
- Add/Swap buttons only appear when window is open
- Staged changelist shown with undo per item
- Warning that unsaved changes will be lost
- 'Window used this week' state when already used

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: gemini-code-assist[bot] <176961590+gemini-code-assist[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Handle cv-qualifiers after typename in deferred alias-template reparse

The reparse block already handles `const typename T` (cv before typename)
but not `typename const T` (cv after typename). parse_type_specifier()
supports both orderings, so mirror that here for consistency.

---------

Fix DashMap deadlock in TxSetTracker eviction

DashMap::iter()/min_by_key() can hold a shard read-lock that conflicts
with remove()'s write-lock on the same shard. Fix by collecting the key
to evict in a separate scope before calling remove.

commit.message:
fix(zfs-install): correct all blocking install-flow issues

- Add -R /mnt/target (altroot) to zpool create so datasets don't clobber
  the live system's root during install
- rpool/var: canmount=noauto during install (bootc rejects pre-mounted /var)
- Remove rpool/home — /home is a symlink to /var/home in this bootc layout
- Add spl_hostid=0x<hostid> karg so generic hostonly=no initramfs can import
  the pool without host-ID mismatch errors on first boot
- Set canmount=on for both datasets after install completes
- Clean zpool export before reboot so pool is importable without -f
- Fix cleanup trap to only unmount EFI + export pool (zpool export handles ZFS)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix LSP client shutdown race causing concurrent PipeReader reads (#267)

StopAsync() was cancelling the notification loop BEFORE sending the
shutdown handshake. When PipeReaderStream.ReadAsync is cancelled, the
underlying PipeReader is left in a dirty state (AdvanceTo never called).
The subsequent SendRequestAsync for 'shutdown' then entered its inline
message-pump path and hit 'Concurrent reads or writes are not
supported' from the corrupted PipeReader.

Fix: send shutdown/exit while the notification loop is still running so
it naturally dispatches the response, then cancel the loop afterwards.
Also wait for the loop task to start before returning from StartAsync so
the first post-init request never races into the inline pump path.

Closes #265, #266

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: ZFS root support in live ISO

- Add --add zfs to live dmsquash-live initramfs so ZFS pools are accessible
  in the live session (requires zfs-dracut installed in base image)
- Deploy zfs-install script to /usr/local/bin/zfs-install in the live image:
  partitions disk (EFI + ZFS), creates rpool with lz4/posixacl/xattr-sa
  datasets (root, var, home), mounts under /mnt/target, runs
  bootc install to-filesystem with root=ZFS=rpool/root kargs
- Add --encrypt flag for aes-256-gcm native ZFS encryption (stretch goal)
- Wire into configure-live.sh; add placeholder desktop entry

Closes #1

feat(replication): add Remote Vault API Key for authenticated sync (#58)

* feat(replication): add Remote Vault API Key field for authenticated sync

Wire up the existing NewClientWithAPIKey() and X-API-Key header support
so that replication sync and test-connection use the configured API key
when connecting to authenticated remote Vault instances.

Backend changes:
- syncRemoteVault() now parses api_key from config JSON and uses
  NewClientWithAPIKey() when set
- TestConnection handler for remote_vault extracts api_key from config
- TestURL handler accepts optional api_key and performs live connectivity
  test when provided

Frontend changes:
- Add 'Remote Vault API Key' password input field below Remote Vault URL
  in the Add/Edit Replication Target modal
- Pass api_key to test-url API call for authenticated connection testing
- API key stored in config JSON blob (same pattern as cloud OAuth creds)

* fix(replication): test connection always performs live connectivity check

TestURL handler now always creates a replication client and calls
TestConnection against the remote Vault server, regardless of whether
an API key is provided. Previously format-only validation was used
when no API key was present.

Updated test to expect 502 for unreachable hosts instead of 200.

* fix(replication): address review feedback on error handling, timeout, and tests

locale: translate missing terms for 15 locales, update english-ok allowlist (#8587)

Translates missing ChurchCRM UI terms across 15 locales (1,941 terms)
and updates `english-ok.json` so the POEditor uploader accepts value=key
entries that are intentionally English (brand names, cognates, locale
names).

## Changes

### Small/medium locales translated inline
- **zh-CN** (5), **zh-TW** (6), **pt-br** (4), **pt** (6), **es / es-AR
/ es-CO / es-MX / es-SV** (7 each), **tr** (6), **fr** (20)
- Terms like `BCC`, `N/A`, `name@example.com` left as `""` per
convention
- `Azure`, `Manual`, `Material`, `Kiosk`, `Malayalam - India`, `Filipino
- Philippines`, colour names etc. translated as value=key (intentionally
English)

### Large locales translated via sub-agents
- **TIER-1:** `hi` (43), `ar` (190), `de` (198), `id` (196), `ja` (190),
`ko` (190), `ru` (189), `vi` (191)
- Denomination-appropriate vocabulary applied per locale (e.g. Coptic
for ar, Lutheran for de, Orthodox for ru)
- Plural forms (`zero/one/two/few/many/other`) filled for Arabic and
Russian

### `english-ok.json` updated
Added entries for all newly translated locales so
`poeditor-upload-missing.js` doesn't skip them as "suspect"
identical-to-source terms:
```json
{
  "tr": ["Azure", "Kiosk", "4. Kiosk"],
  "fr": ["Azure", "Confession", "Format", "Minimum", "Options", ...],
  "es":  ["Azure", "Manual", "Material", "Malayalam - India"],
  ...
}
```

## Remaining (~22 locales, ~3,850 terms)
`af`, `sq`, `am`, `cs`, `nl`, `et`, `fil`, `fi`, `el`, `he`, `hu`, `it`,
`ml`, `nb`, `pl`, `ro`, `sv`, `sw`, `ta`, `te`, `th`, `uk` — not yet
translated. `af` and `sq` translation JSON was produced but not applied
before session ended.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: share-run stability + env lifecycle + CLI surface (v0.4.57)

- Fix1 (share/mod.rs): strip 'sha256:' prefix from ephemeral run root dir
  name to avoid uv/path segment separator crash on production share URLs
- Fix2 (share/mod.rs): remove stale temp root before re-materializing on
  rerun so interrupted runs don't leave non-empty dir errors
- Fix3 (share/mod.rs): fix zero-primary entry ordering bug in interactive
  encap; prompt user to select primary instead of silently falling back
- Fix4 (share/mod.rs): cross-check spec tool_requirements against lock
  resolved_tools in verify_tools to surface missing-tool issues
- dispatch/run.rs + root.rs: add --entry, --env-file, --prompt-env flags
  to 'ato run'; implement env save/reuse lifecycle for share-run targets
- dispatch/mod.rs: plumb new run flags through dispatch layer
- commands/run.rs: add missing 'type=app' to test manifest helper
- lib_tests.rs: fix --via assertion to match updated error message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
3.0 interprocess implementation tag (#380)

* Began work on adding a prefix for the interprocess internal groups

* Finalize ImplementationTag for InterProcessTransporterBase and related classes

- Add 4 new implementation tags in static_group_names.h:
  ZeromqInterprocessTag, ZeromqIntermoduleTag, UdpmInterprocessTag, UdpmIntermoduleTag
- Add include guards and <array> include to static_group_names.h
- Remove default ImplementationTag from InterProcessTransporterBase
- Convert InterProcessForwarder to 2-arg template; add deprecated 1-arg
  specialisation (void tag) with deprecated constructor warning
- Convert InterModuleForwarder to 2-arg template; add deprecated 1-arg
  specialisation (void tag) with deprecated constructor warning
- Update InterModuleTransporterBase alias to 3-arg
- Update InterProcessPortalBase to 3-arg template
- Update InterModulePortalBase to 3-arg template
- Update InterProcessPortalImplementation in zeromq and udpm to 3-arg
  template template parameter
- Add zeromq::InterProcessForwarder and zeromq::InterModuleForwarder aliases
- Add udpm::InterProcessForwarder and udpm::InterModuleForwarder aliases
- Update zeromq/udpm InterProcessPortal and InterModulePortal aliases with
  explicit implementation tags
- Update internal middleware files (simple_thread.h, driver_thread.h,
  driver_thread.cpp, io_transporters.h, io_interface.h) to use explicit
  ZeromqInterprocessTag to avoid deprecation warnings in library code

commit.message:
fix: show team name + owner for free agent pickups in trade history

The isPickup branch rendered 'Picked up X, dropped Y' with no team
context. In the History tab, now shows 'TeamName · Owner picked up X,
dropped Y' so users can see who made each pickup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: fix masternode collateral amounts in CLAUDE.md

Per protocol spec §5.2:
- Free: 0 TIME (was incorrectly implied as 1,000)
- Bronze: 1,000 TIME (was 10,000)
- Silver: 10,000 TIME (was 100,000)
- Gold: 100,000 TIME (was 1,000,000)

Also add sampling weights (Free=1, Bronze=10, Silver=100, Gold=1,000)
which are the actual values used for TimeVote stake weighting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(catalog): remove RETURN trap from library functions; explicit cleanup

Replace trap-based cleanup with explicit if/else so trap side-effects
don't leak into sourcing callers. Also guard mktemp with || return 1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Wire tuna-installer into live env; add flatpak layer to Containerfile

- Containerfile: apt-install flatpak/dbus/rsync; add cached flatpak layer
  that calls install-flatpaks.sh after configure-live.sh; pass INSTALLER_CHANNEL
- configure-live.sh: add SCRIPT_DIR; replace bare polkit rule + INSTALL.txt
  with full tuna-installer wiring: desktop entry override (Ubuntu Installer),
  autostart .desktop, images.json + recipe.json + live-iso-mode copy,
  polkit policy + fisherman symlink, belt-and-suspenders JS rule;
  dconf: add welcome-dialog-last-shown-version=999 + favorite-apps with
  ubuntu-installer.desktop pinned to dock

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
